name: Korean content product review
summary: Reviews Korean reading and media products for account semantics, optional consent, Hangul readability, responsive copy, and accessible playback or reading.

criteria.title: Declare content account password intent
criteria.question: Can password managers distinguish sign-in credentials from password creation?
criteria.evidence: Inspect password inputs for current-password or new-password autocomplete tokens.
verification: Exercise sign-in, signup, and password reset with a platform password manager.
falsePositiveNotes: Passwordless or social-only account flows without password inputs are outside this rule.

criteria.title: Keep content promotions opt-in
criteria.question: Are promotional messages separate from account terms and off by default?
criteria.evidence: Inspect checked checkboxes whose associated label mentions marketing, advertising, or promotional messages.
verification: Open signup in a fresh session and confirm promotional consent is separate and unchecked.
falsePositiveNotes: Do not treat an existing newsletter preference summary as a new opt-in default.

criteria.title: Protect Hangul reading rhythm
criteria.question: Do body size, line-height, measure, paragraph spacing, and font fallback support sustained Korean reading?
criteria.evidence: Capture representative long-form pages at mobile and desktop widths with text scaling and fallback fonts.
verification: Check body line-height, line length, paragraph rhythm, glyph clipping, zoom, and dark-mode contrast.
falsePositiveNotes: Compact captions and metadata can use tighter leading than long-form body text when glyphs remain unclipped.

criteria.title: Keep Korean titles and controls intact across widths
criteria.question: Do long titles, creator names, subtitles, captions, and action labels wrap or truncate without covering controls?
criteria.evidence: Capture longest production-like Korean strings in feeds, detail pages, and players at both declared viewports.
verification: Check title cards, metadata rows, caption controls, text scaling, and Korean IME search.
falsePositiveNotes: Editorial cards may intentionally clamp titles when the full title is available on focus, hover, or the detail page.

criteria.title: Prove reading and playback with assistive technology
criteria.question: Can keyboard and screen-reader users discover, read or play, pause, navigate, and recover without losing position?
criteria.evidence: Attach keyboard order, accessibility-tree, caption, contrast, focus, and saved-position evidence.
verification: Run the approved browser verification profile at mobile and desktop viewports.
falsePositiveNotes: Static control names do not prove media-key behavior, caption synchronization, or saved reading position.
The fixture intentionally omits password autocomplete and preselects marketing consent; corrected account forms must not emit those confirmed findings.

notes: The pack reviews supplied evidence only. It does not fetch protected content, play media, or change reading history.
